original: A timeless classic from Earth carefully fitted with a covert microtransmitter. Consisting of a jacket, button-up shirt, tie, and trousers, it lends a veneer of respectability to all cloak-and-dagger aficionados and stool pigeons.

[flags.VELIKA_DHAAL_APPROACH |[var0 |Velika’s eyes furrow and as you approach, fixing you with a disapproving stare. “Tch you ought to take better care of yourself, innie.” She leans forward, gently touching your injured form. |Velika leans back in her chair as you approach, a smirk tugging at the corner of her mouth. “Glad you could join me, innie.” ] |Velika’s gaze flicks to yours as you slide into an adjacent stool, her expression unreadable. “I haven’t managed to scare you off yet?” She chuckles mirthlessly. “That’s a first.” She takes a long drag on her cigarette, letting the smoke escape her lips and waft between the two of you. “You’re crazy to hang around someone like me. I’m glad you’re here, innie.” “Still calling me ‘innie’?” you huff. “And here I thought I finally achieved outer status.” “It’s a force of habit. ‘Innie’ just really rolls off the tongue, you know?” The cyborg smirks and leans forward, tracing a finger along your jawline. “Besides, you’re aiming to become head of Steele Tech–[pc.mf |King |Queen ] of the Innies.” Her lips curve into a small frown. “Hopefully... you won’t just become another boring suit when that happens. The cyborg cocks her head at your approach, her prosthetic eyes gleaming with calculation. “Well [pc.name], what are you going to do about that schmoozefest the zaibatsus are throwing? [var0 |Your current threads are nice but deserve better, don’t you think?” |“Have an outfit lined up already?” ] “How do you know about the party?” you inquire, arching your eyebrows. “And why do you care?” “A little bird told me. “Unfortunate,” the cyborg sighs, lighting up a cigarette and taking a long drag of it. “But you’ve done so much for me already. I can’t fault you for not wanting to go along with another one of my schemes.”

original: The cyborgs pauses for a moment, [pc.ra zaika |her expression almost curious as she takes in your genemodded form, studying you. |studying you closely in a manner you’ve long since come to associate with her. ] “Their proficiency with cybernetics is... interesting,” she remarks finally. “They’re not as good as the Gabilani of course but not every species is capable of navigating their way into modernity as smoothly as they have. Still...” She wrinkles her nose. “Dhaal is a goddamn cesspool, a bordello crammed with every kind of lowlife, scumbag and innie tourist you can imagine. And don’t even get me started on their dietary preferences.” She sighs, fishing a pack of Gryvain filters from her pocket and lighting one with practiced ease. “You know, [var0 |Dhaal is a lot like how Myrellion was before...” her face darkens, a muscle in her jaw twitching involuntarily. “Megacorps are looking to bleed the planet dry and the ruling elite will just stand by and let it happen. It’s a tragedy as old as the Planet Rush itself.” She raises her brows, watching to see if you’re following. “And there’s not a damn thing anyone can do about it.” |Dhaal is in the same boat as Myrellion —megacorps are looking to bleed the planet dry and the ruling elite will just stand by and let it happen. It’s a tragedy as old as the Planet Rush itself.” She raises her brows, watching to see if you’re following. “And there’s not a damn thing anyone can do about it.” ] You cock an eyebrow, commenting on Velika’s apparent pessimism. “I’m just being realistic, [pc.name]. We have all this technology and wealth yet we’re still not the masters of our own destinies. The forces that shape our lives are always just one step ahead.” She sighs, wiping her mouth with the back of her prosthetic hand. “I always get this way when I drink too much...”

“Her taste in guns makes me sick,” the cyborg replies, drumming her fingers against her prosthetic arms. “Like most Ts, she doesn’t understand that a true pistol is an art form; something to master not merely to fire. An Eagle Industries handgun might be fine for civilians or police patrolmen who only fire 50 rounds a year but at the end of the day they’re bricks given plastic and tool steel form. Compare that to one of my flechetters: when I raise it to my eye, it lines up naturally and it emits such a strong round it could kill a saurmorian. They’re elegant weapons.”\n\n\t“Uh-huh,” you reply,

“And what do you think of her personally?” “She’s surprisingly resourceful seeing as she’s managed to stay alive this long. But that could easily change for an unmodded human. Mercenary work doesn’t give second chances.” Velika’s prosthetic eyes narrow and she leans forward. “Why do you ask, huh?” She runs her hand along your jaw, her thumb brushing possessively against the sensitive [pc.skinFurScalesNounSimple] of your throat. “Don’t tell me you’re looking to hit it off with the gun nut.” [flags.EVENING_SEXED |[var0 |You smirk and cheerfully inform her |You quietly inform her that ] you’ve already done far more than just ‘hit it off’ with Evening, prompting her to scoff. “Typical,” she scoffs before regarding Evening from afar, her prosthetic eyes raking over her curves approvingly. “At least you don’t have terrible taste...” |You shrug and reply that you were just curious, prompting Velika to cock an eyebrow. “Just curious... right...” She exhales a puff of smoke from her cigarette before lapsing into silence. ]

“The Vorician Salvation Army,” the cyborg replies, her tone one of studied indifference. “Look [pc.name]...” She removes a stick from a crumpled package of cigarettes and places it between her lips. As she lights it, she takes a leisurely drag before resuming to speak. “I can’t tell you. Literally.” She gestures towards the sockets clustered on the back of her neck. “And believe me when I say that you’re better off not knowing.” Velika pauses for a moment, her eyes fixed intently on her face. “I know that’s not the answer you’re looking for... but I hope you believe me when I say that I love you and I don’t desert my own.” She moves a prosthetic hand up to cradle your cheek, her touch surprisingly gentle. You change your line of inquiry, asking Velika what the intentions of her shadowy employers are towards yourself. The blonde tilts her head to the side slightly, her prosthetic eyes bright with calculation. “I imagine they feel... surprised by your willingness to overlook my... transgressions. And that they hope I can act as a go-between you and them in the future.” “So you’re saying they’ll contact me in the future?” you ask, cocking an eyebrow. “What exactly do they want from me?” “Your... cooperation,” Velika taps some ash off her cigarette before continuing. “Megacorps have lots of enemies. And many of Steele Tech’s enemies happen to be our enemies.” She pauses, searching your face for agreement. “I can’t offer anything concrete right now. But give it some thought... we’d be unstoppable working together, don’t you think?”

“Oh? Sure you wanna talk about them again, innie?” Velika presses herself up against you, her hand slipping under your [pc.upperGarmentOuter], moving her fingers in slow, tantalizing circles against your [pc.skinFurScalesNounSimple]. “Well if you must know, my relationship with the reds was one of convenience. They were desperate to cultivate foreign connections of their own after the Gilden Republic started courting Xenogen and the like. My benefactors and I simply couldn’t let such a... golden opportunity go to waste. A few shipments of ‘humanitarian’ aid were all that was needed for us to gain a valuable foothold on Myrellion.” She cocks her head, studying your face. “The plain yet hard truth is that the myr aren’t in control of their planet anymore. Their affairs will be directed by suits in air-conditioned boardrooms halfway across the galaxy long after the warfleet circling over their heads is gone.”

[flags.VELIKA_DHAAL_TLK_REL |“You wanna talk about us again?” she scoffs, leaning in closer to you. “Fine. What’s on your mind, lover [pc.boy]?” She reaches out a hand, walking two fingers up your chest. You ask Velika if she’s ever thought of what the future holds for the two of you, prompting her to nearly spit out her whisky. “The future? I’m not the type of gal to settle down or anything, [pc.name]. There’s no domestic side beneath all the complications and violence, just a broken soldier who should have died years ago.” She silences your nascent protests by capturing your mouth in a brutal, devouring kiss, worrying your bottom lip with her teeth as she plunders the warm cavern of your mouth with her tongue. The cyborg pulls back slightly after a few beats, her forehead resting against yours, her breath mingling with your own. “I care about you, [pc.name]... believe me I do. But I doubt either of us is seriously going to give up the spacer lifestyle anytime soon. We’re both too wild and untamed.” |Velika tilts her head to gaze up at you, a soft, almost vulnerable look in her eyes. “I really don’t know what you see in me, [pc.name]. I’m a total cynical bitch after all. A monster. And you’re...

She takes your hand in hers, bringing it to her lips to press a tender, reverent kiss to your palm. “I’m used to being alone... but I don’t want to be alone anymore. I want to be with you.” She stares up at you for a long moment before crossing her arms over her chest. “Alright, that’s enough mushy stuff. I’m glad we’re... together... but don’t expect me to start acting like a simpering teenage girl. I have a reputation to maintain.” You tell Velika you’d never expect that from her, prompting her to grin. “Good.” She leans forward, her hands sliding down your body to grip your [pc.ass]. “Don’t expect me to be gentle either. I’m rough, demanding and I expect my [pc.man] to keep up with me.” ]

Velika grabs your [pc.hair] roughly, yanking your head back to force you to look at her as she stares down at you with a fierce, almost warning glare. “You don’t seem to understand what that entails so let me spell it out for you: being with me will ruin your life. You’ll have to give up everything and I mean everything. No more palling around with the galaxy’s super rich, no more fancy dinners on Terra and no more Steele Tech...” She leans in closer, her face mere inches from yours, her breath hot and heavy against your skin. “Do you think you can live with that? Do you think you can endure being a galactic pariah? Don’t be an idiot, [pc.name]. Don’t throw away your entire life for me.”

A low, relieved sound escapes Velika’s lips at your words, her expression softening. She leans her forehead against yours, her breath mingling with your own as she speaks. “Good. You’ve got a destiny to fulfill, a company to run. This is what’s best for you... and for us.” She leans in to press a soft, lingering kiss to your lips. “I love you, [pc.name]. Never forget that.”

“Hello, comrades!” “Hello, Comrade Marshal!” “I congratulate you on the four hundredth and seventh anniversary of Independence Day!” “Hurraaaa!” Velika looks down on your joined hands, a soft smile playing at the corners of her lips as soldiers decked out in wave-green uniforms and ceremonial phrygian caps march past your shared viewing platform. Your elopement with Velika and subsequent defection to the Icarian Commonwealth did indeed cost you everything. Your friends, acquaintances and the galaxy at large can’t fathom why you’d willingly give up any chance of becoming CEO of Steele Tech in favor of throwing your lot in with the “hermit kingdom of the outer rim.” But as your cyborg lover gives your fingers a gentle squeeze, any lingering doubts you might have had are swept away by a tidal wave of raw, unbridled love and contentment. Let others waste their lives committing mammonistic crimes and tyrannizing their fellow man. This is where you belong. The future is bright. The future is red.

As you navigate the neon-dappled streets of Dhaal, the air thick with smog and distant sounds of revelry, you suddenly feel the bite of cold metal pressed up against your [pc.skinFurScalesNounSimple]. “Hey there, lover [pc.boy]. We have unfinished business.” You crane your head, confirming without a doubt who exactly has accosted you. “Velika? What the hell are you doing?” The cyborg chuckles darkly. “What does it look like I’m doing, innie? I’m tying up a loose end.” You hear the mechanical click of a hammer being cocked. “You should have known better than to... relax I’m just fucking with you.” She retracts the metal from your spine, much to your relief. “It’s good to see you again, [pc.name].” She turns you about, pressing her forehead against yours, her blonde hair falling around you like a curtain. “I missed you. And as much as I’d love to drag you to a dark corner somewhere and... catch up, I’ve got a few things to knock off my to-do list while I’m on this rock. Come and find me at the Crash Landing later. I promise I’ll make it worth your while.” She wraps her arms around you, drawing you into a brief hug before heading off into the distance.

You spot Velika seated near the bar. She makes no indication of having noticed you, her slender prosthetic fingers tracing up and down along the condensation of her glass as a cigarette dangles from her mouth.

Zero Plus is a curvy zaika slightly over 7' tall. Her long, tapered ears surface from cascades of stringy locks that frame her head. She’s got bright, bubbly eyes burning with naive curiosity — like a small child’s. Small, round glasses rest on her nose. Considering how little light they filter, she must have either very good diurnal vision for a zaika or her eyes are so full of stars that she doesn’t mind the strong daylight. She’s got [silly
		|Dick-Sucking Lips

She’s wearing a long, hooded coat over a revealing bodysuit that struggles to contain her fat, sloshing tits and bubble butt, a pair of short gloves, and thigh high socks — all branded with red crosses. The inside of the coat glows in the dark, ensuring that she’s visible at all times. Dr. Ovvi [silly

	] modified her chest and tail so that it could store and inject microsurgeon-laced mixtures into unlucky test subjects or willing patients. 
	
	When she’s aroused, her syringe-like bulb opens to reveal a more conventional zaika tail tip, but nothing stops her from using her narrow end to pump someone full of highly-pressurized medicinal spunk. Two large handlebars frame her hips, highlighting her waspish waist and providing you with support if you ever want to bring her to pound town.
